description_long: This object consists of two distinct grave markers set on a rectangular concrete or stone base. On the left, there is a rectangular chest tomb with a flat lid, weathered with moss and dark stains, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. On the right, an obelisk-style monument rises from a circular stone border, featuring engraved panels that may once have held inscriptions, though they are now largely unreadable. The obelisk is tall and slender, with a pointed top and visible weathering, while the chest tomb is more squat and solid. The base is a flat, paved surface, likely concrete or stone, with some dirt and debris around the monuments. The overall appearance is somber and aged, typical of a small urban graveyard plot.
description: A weathered graveyard plot featuring a rectangular chest tomb and a tall obelisk monument on a stone base, both showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: View of the chest tomb on the left and the obelisk monument on the right, both set on a rectangular stone base.
description_view_1: Obelisk monument is prominent in the foreground, showing engraved panels, with the chest tomb visible behind it.
description_view_2: Rear view of the obelisk and the back of the chest tomb, highlighting the flat surfaces and weathered textures.
description_view_3: Chest tomb is in the foreground with its mossy lid, while the obelisk stands behind, partially obscured.
